methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $19M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Foundry Robotics is a Seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company led by Adarsh Kulkarni that has raised $19M to date. The robotics and manufacturing sector includes 9 comparable companies: 1 public (277810.KQ) and 8 private peers, including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity.

Valuation analysis is constrained by limited comparable data at Foundry's stage and tier. The peer-median method could not be applied due to insufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables within the robotics sector—while 7 same-sector peers exist, none satisfied the minimum threshold of 3 datapoints within relevant filters. The stage-progression method, using $19M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 2.5x Seed-to-next-stage step-up multiplier, yields an implied valuation of $48M. This method remains the primary anchoring approach available given current data constraints.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $40M to $55M, with a median of $48M. This single-method triangulation carries material uncertainty: the range reflects a 15% discount and 15% premium around the median to account for early-stage variance and the absence of transaction-level peer validation. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.5x multiplier to Foundry's specific risk profile and the lack of direct peer transaction data at equivalent stages. Additional comparable transactions or progress toward Series A would substantially improve precision.
